messages.content: Yo just wanted to share a bug I found in the playground, if you are testing a current sheet called example1 and you continue to use the edit feature on sheet example1 instead of creating a new test sheet for your updated version, if you are constantly editing example1 sheet, even with the updated version edited into example1 this sheet will carry over past pollution data from your prior version of example1 ... I found it best to always create a new sheet in the playground.. for example if in example1 it was constantly asking for verdict (f) or (t) and you change that via edit to (false) or (true) you will still receive some prompt returns with (f) or (t) while sometimes (false) or (true) it may take a bunch of tests to get full (false) or (true) compliance again.. if you created a new sheet all first prompt returns would have had (false) or (true) only... If it's that obvious with just swapping (f)/(t) to (true)/(false) who knows what other polluted data also holds if your edits are larger..
messages.subject: Playground sheet edit pollution bug
